paper_title: A growing degree day inference model based on mountain birch leaf cuticle analysis over a latitudinal gradient in Fennoscandia

methods.description: This method involves collecting and analyzing meteorological data to compare with cuticle analysis results. It includes extracting temperature and precipitation data from the nearest weather stations and calculating growing degree days (GDD 5) and other climatic parameters.

steps.description: The measured daily average temperature and precipitation data of the weather station nearest to each individual sampling site was extracted from the KNMI Explorer database.
steps.searchable_summary: extract daily temperature and precipitation data from KNMI Explorer database

steps.description: GDD 5 was calculated from daily temperatures recorded throughout the growing season.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate GDD 5 from daily temperature data

steps.description: The sum of GDD 5 was calculated from 1 January 2016 to 30 September 2016.
steps.searchable_summary: sum GDD 5 values for specified date range

steps.description: May through September (MJJAS) temperature and precipitation data were used to calculate average spring and summer conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate average temperature and precipitation for MJJAS

steps.description: Average winter temperature and precipitation were calculated from November, December 2015 as well as January, February and March 2016 (NDJFM).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate average winter temperature and precipitation for NDJFM

steps.description: Average precipitation and the total precipitation sum were calculated as 1 October 2015 until 30 September 2016.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate average and total precipitation for specified date range

steps.description: PP was defined as the cumulative sunlight hours at the latitude of each sample location between March and September.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate cumulative sunlight hours for specified latitude and date range

methods.description: This method involves developing a GDD 5 inference model using linear regression to relate UI values to known GDD 5 values across sampling localities. The model is tested for predictive accuracy using independent data.

steps.description: Linear least-square regression was used to relate measured UI values to known GDD 5 across all sampling localities.
steps.searchable_summary: perform linear regression to relate UI values to GDD 5

steps.description: Using inverse regression, we obtained the linear function [GDD 5 ] = 7744.8[UI] −8086.8.
steps.searchable_summary: derive linear function from inverse regression

steps.description: To test the predictive skills and accuracy of the inference model, independent UI data from a transect with a similar latitudinal range collected in 1997 with known GDD 5 at growth locality have been run through the inference model.
steps.searchable_summary: validate inference model using independent UI data
